frozen: ground truth annotated manually by the author after reading the chat, BEFORE writing any extractor; never modified afterwards

claims.what: the reproducibility test failure is caused by list(set(...)) being non-deterministic across runs when PYTHONHASHSEED differs
CI does not fix PYTHONHASHSEED; local environment does
the 12-vs-3-minute CI/local benchmark discrepancy is currently unexplained

constraints.what: do not pin PYTHONHASHSEED globally as a workaround; that masks other latent non-determinism

decisions.what: fix the determinism bug by sorting the list before hashing instead of pinning PYTHONHASHSEED
add a regression check (grep-style) to the determinism scanner that no list-from-set passes through the hashing path
do not profile or fix the slow-CI benchmark issue now, just note the discrepancy

conflicts.what: CI wants fully deterministic side-effect-free tests, but some integration tests intentionally hit the filesystem cache (currently masked as flaky-tolerant)

open_questions.what: are there downstream callers that depend on the original (unsorted) list order from the hashing function
why is the auditor benchmark 12 minutes on CI but 3 minutes locally
